headlines.title: Leadership uncertainty 'enormously disruptive', former top civil servant warns
headlines.description: Speaking on Sunday with Laura Kuenssberg, the former Cabinet Secretary said leadership transitions are "enormously disruptive".

headlines.title: US-Iran talks to begin in Switzerland as Tehran says it closed Strait of Hormuz
headlines.description: The US disputed Iran's claim the waterway is shut, a move Tehran says was a response to Israeli attacks in Lebanon.

headlines.title: Extreme heat warning expanded as 37C heatwave approaches UK
headlines.description: After a slightly cooler Saturday, temperatures will rise again, possibly touching 36C by Tuesday with a Met Office amber extreme heat warning set to come into force.

headlines.title: Man charged after suspected anti-Muslim attacks in Edinburgh
headlines.description: Two people were injured close to a mosque and a man was later seen battering the door of a pizzeria as members of the public run away.

headlines.title: Jeremy Clarkson in remission from prostate cancer
headlines.description: The presenter shared his "aggressive" cancer diagnosis on an episode of Clarkson's Farm earlier this week.

headlines.title: Meet the Curacao keeper who made record 15 saves to keep Ecuador out
headlines.description: Curacao's Eloy Room makes a record-equalling 15 saves to help his tiny island nation to a first World Cup point against Ecuador.

headlines.title: Dutch royals enjoy two big results in one day
headlines.description: There may be about 5,000 miles between the Netherlands and their former colony Curacao but members of the Dutch royal family managed to see both sides play on Saturday.

headlines.title: How many World Cup Golden Boot winners can you name?
headlines.description: How many World Cup top goalscorers from down the years can you name?

headlines.title: How the social media ban could reshape how all of us use the internet
headlines.description: Why some argue the social media ban could have a profound affect on how young people gain new knowledge and the rest of us move around online

headlines.title: Tequila spritz and dot cakes - why these summer trends are having a moment
headlines.description: Fairy cakes, milk bread and a fresh take on a popular cocktail will be big in the coming months.

headlines.title: Backstage at Gorillaz' epic, one-off stadium show: 'The vibe is ridiculous'
headlines.description: Damon Albarn, De La Soul and Moonchild Sannelly talk backstage as Gorillaz play their biggest show.

headlines.title: True crime's biggest convention faces its own moral dilemma
headlines.description: Victims' families say there are ways to be an ethical true-crime fan - not an exploitative one.

headlines.title: Former Olympian denies vandalising Washington Reflecting Pool after arrest
headlines.description: Davey Hearn says he was simply touching the new paint at the site out of curiosity and did not remove or alter it.

headlines.title: King Charles to reveal personal tax bill for first time as monarch
headlines.description: For the first time, the annual financial report for the royals will include how much tax is paid by the monarch.

headlines.title: Nine people in critical condition among 100 injured in Bedford train crash
headlines.description: British Transport Police says the collision, which killed a train driver, is being investigated.

headlines.title: Three men die after White City building fire
headlines.description: The men were rescued from the single-storey building but died soon after, London Fire Brigade said.

headlines.title: Fuel sales halted in occupied Crimea as Ukraine targets oil facilities
headlines.description: Fuel had already been rationed due to shortages caused by Kyiv's attacks against supply routes in Russian-occupied territories.

headlines.title: Tom Hanks congratulates boy, 8, on Toy Story 5 artwork
headlines.description: Tommy, from Blackpool, will have his artwork shown at the UK screenings of the new Disney Pixar film.

headlines.title: 'He's a life saver': The miniature therapy horse that travels by taxi
headlines.description: Meet Rusty, the pint-sized therapy horse who travels around London in a black cab.

headlines.title: Oldest Birthday Honours recipient is 'delighted'
headlines.description: Pat Knifton Hough is recognised for 57 years being involved with Nantwich Players in Cheshire.

headlines.title: Eight police pups named after Three Lions stars
headlines.description: It is hoped the eight puppies will emulate the success of their namesakes.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Burnham Wins in Makerfield - So What Happens Now?
headlines.description: Burnham wins Makerfield by-election, paving way for leadership challenge against Starmer.

headlines.title: Jill Biden reveals how she really felt about Joe Biden’s health whilst he was president
headlines.description: And Trump wants to give America’s top legal job to his former personal lawyer

headlines.title: Two chancers bluff their way into weddings to meet women
headlines.description: Owen Wilson and Vince Vaughn star in this classic 2005 comedy

headlines.title: Doku criticised over plan to return home for birth
headlines.description: Winger Jeremy Doku is criticised for saying he wants to leave Belgium's World Cup camp to be with his wife when she gives birth to their first child.

headlines.title: All three England keepers played there - Carlisle's role in trio's rise
headlines.description: From west Cumbria to the World Cup, Carlisle is England's goalkeeper factory, with all of Thomas Tuchel's goalkeepers passing through the club.

headlines.title: How Murray was tempted back to tennis - and would he ever do a Serena?
headlines.description: British tennis icon Andy Murray discusses returning to coaching with Jack Draper, Serena Williams' comeback and filling the void of not playing.

headlines.title: Mark Hughes' son Alex dies aged 38
headlines.description: Wales legend Mark Hughes says he is "totally heartbroken" after the death of his son Alex aged 38.
